title: Warrior Trading Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Warrior Trading Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Warrior Trading (3.8★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 202
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Warrior Trading — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Warrior Trading?

Warrior Trading is a day trading education platform founded in 2012 by Ross Cameron, a trader with a verified track record in momentum and small-cap trading. The platform combines live streaming chat rooms during market hours, structured multi-level courses, real-time trade alerts, and a realistic paper trading simulator designed specifically for aspiring day traders. With a 3.8/5 rating on TradingToolsHub, Warrior Trading attracts traders who want to learn momentum strategies, scalping techniques, and small-cap trading tactics in a community-driven environment. The platform operates on a freemium model—you can start with the free Starter tier—but the full experience requires upgrading to Warrior Pro ($197/month) or Inner Circle ($297/month).

How to Create Your Warrior Trading Account

Getting started with Warrior Trading takes about 10 minutes. Here's the step-by-step process:

  • Visit the website and click "Sign Up" on the homepage. You'll be directed to a registration form.
  • Enter your email address and create a password. Warrior Trading doesn't require extensive personal information for the free tier, but you'll need a valid email to confirm your account.
  • Verify your email by clicking the confirmation link sent to your inbox. This step typically takes less than a minute.
  • Choose your plan. You can immediately access the free Starter tier or upgrade to Warrior Pro or Inner Circle during setup. Many users start free and upgrade later after exploring the platform.
  • Complete your profile by adding your trading experience level (beginner, intermediate, advanced). This helps the platform tailor course recommendations and live chat room access.
  • Link your brokerage account (optional). Warrior Trading integrates with most major brokers via read-only connections. This isn't required for using the simulator, but it allows you to track real trades alongside your paper trading activity.
  • Download the mobile app if you plan to monitor alerts and chat rooms on the go. Warrior Trading offers iOS and Android apps with full functionality.

Total estimated setup time: 10–15 minutes from registration to your first live chat session.

Setting Up Warrior Trading for the First Time

When you log in for the first time, Warrior Trading presents a clean, chat-focused dashboard. The main screen is split into three sections: the live chat room on the left (where Ross Cameron and other veteran traders call out trades in real-time during market hours), a video feed or news ticker in the center, and your watchlist and alerts panel on the right.

Dashboard Configuration Steps:

  • Set your market hours timezone. Warrior Trading operates primarily during US stock market hours (9:30 AM–4:00 PM ET). In Settings, confirm your timezone so alerts and chat room opening times display correctly in your local time.
  • Enable push notifications for trade alerts. Go to Alerts Settings and toggle on notifications for specific patterns (gaps, pre-market movers, small-cap breakouts). You can customize alert frequency to avoid notification fatigue.
  • Configure your watchlist. Add 5–10 stocks you want to monitor based on Warrior Trading's curated small-cap screeners. The platform provides pre-made watchlists for different strategies (gap-ups, breakouts, reversals) that you can customize.
  • Set your paper trading account balance. Warrior Trading's simulator lets you start with $25,000 (required for day trading under Pattern Day Trader rules) or any amount you choose. Higher balances let you test bigger positions; lower balances simulate the reality of a small trading account.
  • Join your experience-level chat room. Warrior Trading segregates beginners from advanced traders. Select "Beginner Chat" or "Advanced Chat" based on your experience. You can switch later, but beginners benefit from focused discussions without high-frequency jargon.
  • Download the trading journal tool. Warrior Trading provides a downloadable spreadsheet or integrates with third-party journal apps. Start logging trades immediately, even in your simulator, to build the habit of reviewing your performance.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading Simulator

Warrior Trading's simulator runs on real market data with realistic execution speeds. You can practice momentum strategies, test position sizing, and build confidence without risking capital. The simulator tracks your win rate, average profit/loss per trade, and risk-to-reward ratios—metrics Ross Cameron emphasizes in his teaching. Unlike some platforms that fill all orders instantly, Warrior Trading simulates realistic slippage and market conditions, making the practice experience closer to live trading.

2. Live Chat Room During Market Hours

The heartbeat of Warrior Trading is its live chat room. Ross Cameron (or other senior traders when Ross isn't streaming) calls out potential trades in real-time: "Gap-up on ABCD, watch for breakout above $5.20," or "XYZ reversing off the 50-day, setting up for a short." You see the exact entry, profit target, and stop loss. The key advantage: you learn by watching live decision-making, not replayed videos. The chat is active 9:30 AM–4:00 PM ET on trading days; some premium tiers include extended pre-market chat (7:00–9:30 AM).

3. Real-Time Trade Alerts

Warrior Trading scans for specific setups—gap-ups, small-cap breakouts, and reversals—and pushes alerts to your phone or email. Alerts arrive with the stock ticker, the trigger price, and the setup name (e.g., "ABCD gap-up above $5 resistance"). You can customize alert sensitivity to reduce noise. The advantage over generic screeners: Warrior Trading's alerts are tuned for the exact momentum patterns Ross Cameron teaches, not generic volume spikes.

4. Structured Multi-Level Curriculum

Warrior Trading isn't just a chat room; it's a structured trading school. The curriculum spans 10+ courses organized by skill level: Beginner (stock market fundamentals, chart patterns), Intermediate (momentum strategy, risk management, position sizing), and Advanced (high-frequency day trading, micro-cap strategies). Each course includes video lessons, quizzes, and live Q&A sessions. Courses are evergreen—you access them on-demand at any time, on any device.

5. Mobile App with Full Functionality

The Warrior Trading mobile app isn't stripped-down; it includes live chat, alerts, watchlist, and paper trading access. You can monitor trades from your commute, receive alerts, and even place practice trades on your phone. The chat room interface works smoothly on mobile, though most power users prefer desktop for executing the actual trades.

6. Risk Management and Position Sizing Tools

Warrior Trading bakes risk management into every lesson and tool. The position sizing calculator helps you determine trade size based on account balance, risk per trade (usually 1–2%), and stop-loss distance. This is critical: Ross Cameron emphasizes that 90% of trading success is risk management, not entry timing. The platform logs your risk metrics (risk-to-reward ratio, win percentage, expectancy) in your trade journal, forcing you to confront whether your strategy is actually profitable.

7. Community Features and Accountability

Warrior Trading includes forums, Discord-like chat channels organized by strategy or skill level, and weekly community challenges where traders compete (virtually, not for money) on strategy execution. This social component keeps motivation high and provides peer support when you hit losing streaks.

Warrior Trading Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Starter Tier: Free

The free tier gives you access to live chat rooms (read-only in some streams), limited course access (intro courses only), and the paper trading simulator. You won't receive real-time alerts or access to advanced courses. Free is ideal for deciding if day trading appeals to you before paying.

Warrior Pro: $197/month (or ~$997/year if paid annually)

Warrior Pro unlocks the full curriculum, real-time alerts, write access to the live chat (so you can ask questions Ross will see), and advanced chat room features. Most traders start here after the free tier. Warrior Pro is best for intermediate traders serious about learning momentum strategies but not yet ready for the highest tier.

Inner Circle: $297/month (or ~$1,200/year annually)

Inner Circle adds one-on-one email support from Ross Cameron's team, exclusive advanced courses (micro-cap strategies, high-frequency techniques), and early access to new features. This tier is for traders who want direct feedback on their trades and strategies. Inner Circle pays off if you're trading with real money and want expert guidance on refining your approach.

Which Plan for Your Skill Level?

  • Complete Beginner: Start with the free Starter tier for 2–3 weeks. Watch the intro courses and lurk in chat. If you're excited, upgrade to Warrior Pro.
  • Intermediate (Some trading experience, ready to focus on day trading): Jump straight to Warrior Pro. The alerts, full courses, and chat access are worth the $197/month if day trading is your focus.
  • Advanced (Already profitable, want to scale or refine your edge): Inner Circle makes sense if you're trading with real money and the 1-on-1 feedback accelerates your improvement. Otherwise, Warrior Pro is sufficient.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Warrior Trading

1. Trade the Same Setups Ross Calls in Live Chat

Don't try to invent your own strategy. In your first 30 days, replicate the exact setups Ross calls: the entry price, profit target, stop loss, and exit rules. Log 20+ trades of the same setup in your simulator. Only after you've proven consistency with one setup should you experiment with others. This focused approach prevents "shiny object syndrome" and builds a real edge.

2. Join the Beginner Chat Even If You Have Experience

Intermediate or advanced traders sometimes skip beginner chat out of ego. Don't. Beginner chat moves slower, questions are more thoughtful, and Ross often takes time to explain the "why" behind decisions. You'll pick up nuances you'd miss in the fast-paced advanced room. You can switch back after a week or two.

3. Use the Trade Journal Daily—Review Every Week

Logging trades is mandatory if you want to improve. But the real work is the weekly review: calculate your win rate, average profit/loss per trade, best and worst trades, and emotional mistakes (did you panic-sell winners or hold losers too long?). Warrior Trading provides templates for this, but you have to do the work. Traders who skip this step plateau after 3–6 months.

4. Set Alerts for Pre-Market Movers 30 Minutes Before Open

The best day trading setups form in the first 30 minutes of the market open. Warrior Trading's alerts identify gap-ups and reversals, but you need 15–20 minutes before 9:30 AM ET to scan these stocks, watch for the breakout setup, and prepare your order. Set an alarm to wake up, check pre-market movers, and be ready when the bell rings. This single habit will improve your edge significantly.

5. Ignore the Upsell Courses and Stay Disciplined on Your Plan

Warrior Trading occasionally promotes advanced "secrets" courses or mastermind groups. Resist them initially. Master the core momentum curriculum (Gap-ups, Reversals, Breakouts) for 3 months first. You can't execute advanced strategies if you haven't nailed the basics. Upsells prey on traders desperate for quick wins, not on traders building real skills.

6. Paper Trade Until You Hit 65% Win Rate Consistently

Warrior Trading doesn't require you to simulator trade, but Ross Cameron's coaching is clear: don't trade with real money until you've proven a 60–65% win rate over 50+ trades in your simulator. That's roughly 6–8 weeks of disciplined paper trading. Jumping to real money before this is expensive tuition.

7. Attend the Weekly Live Q&A—Ask Specific Trade Questions

Warrior Pro and Inner Circle members get live Q&A sessions with Ross or senior traders. Asking "How do I get better?" is useless. Instead, bring a specific trade you took (even a losing one) with a chart, your entry and exit, and ask why the setup failed or worked. These targeted questions get actionable feedback and train your eye for pattern recognition.

Common Warrior Trading Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "I'm Not Receiving Alerts"

Check three things: (1) your notification settings are enabled in the app and browser, (2) your timezone is set correctly (alerts filter by market hours), and (3) your alert criteria are not too strict. If alerts are set to only "gap-ups above 50%," you'll miss most days. Loosen criteria to "gap-ups above 20%" initially, then tighten as you improve. If alerts still fail, restart the app and clear the cache.

Issue 2: "The Chat Room is Too Fast—I Can't Keep Up"

The live chat moves in real-time, and it can feel overwhelming. Solution: mute the chat during your first 5 sessions and watch the video feed of Ross's screen instead. You'll see the setups he's calling without the sensory overload of 100+ traders asking questions. Once you recognize the patterns visually, unmute chat and engage. Or, watch the replay videos later at 1.25x speed so you can pause and think.

Issue 3: "I'm Losing Money on Paper Trades and Feel Discouraged"

Losing on a simulator feels fake, but it's exactly the feedback loop you need. If you're underwater on paper, you'll be destroyed on real capital. Instead of feeling discouraged, treat losses as data: review the trade journal, identify the pattern (Did you enter too late? Exit too early? Violate your stop loss?), and adjust. After 5–10 losing trades on the same pattern, switch to a different setup. The market rewards persistence, not stubbornness.

Issue 4: "I'm Tempted to Trade Outside Market Hours"

Warrior Trading's chat room and alerts are synchronized to US stock market hours (9:30 AM–4:00 PM ET). If you're trading cryptocurrency, forex, or futures outside these hours and losing, that's expected—you're not following the system Warrior Trading teaches. Stick to the US stock market during the hours Warrior Trading operates. Extended hours (pre-market and after-hours) have much wider spreads and lower volume, making setups less reliable.

Is Warrior Trading Worth It? Our Verdict

Warrior Trading is worth it if you're serious about day trading and willing to put in the time to learn the system. Ross Cameron's verified track record, the structured curriculum, the live chat accountability, and the realistic simulator create an environment where disciplined traders can build a real edge. The 3.8/5 rating reflects the fact that Warrior Trading works—if you work. At $197/month for Warrior Pro, you're paying for access to a profitable mentor's real-time decision-making and a community that holds you accountable to risk management, both of which accelerate your learning far beyond self-study. However, Warrior Trading is not worth it if you expect it to be easy, if you're not willing to spend 6–8 weeks paper trading before going live, or if you're looking for "secret strategies" without doing the work. Compare to Benzinga if you want alerts without the live chat coaching, or TradingView if you need powerful charting but less education. For aspiring day traders with the discipline to execute, Warrior Trading delivers real value. For traders looking for shortcuts, look elsewhere.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
